div.p: Let all present and future know that I, William, son of Ralph the Lardener, and brother David, his sons, and their heir, have quiclaimed and abjured for myself and for my heirs, and resigned by rod and staff, to Robert de Brus and his heirs, or those to whom they may have wished to assign, the whole land which the aforesaid Ralph and the said David and my predecessors and I have held of the aforesaid Robert de Brus and his predecessors, and every right which belonged to us within the toun of Annan and within the dependent area; in return for the account, that is to say, of David my brother, who at that time was the sergeand of the lord Robert de Brus in 'Herterville'; the account which although I, William, undertook to settle on behalf the said David my brother, I was not able to pay off; and in return for the hundred shillings which the aforesaid lord Robert de Brus proffered to me. In testimony of this quitclaim and abjuration I have applied my seal to this writing. With these witnesses: the lord Richard of Levington; the lord Roger Avenel; William de Brus; John de Brus; William de Heriz; Humphrey de Jardin; Hugh of Corrie; Robert of Crosby; Gilbert son of John; Roger of Kirkpatrick; Robert of Tremore; Richard de Bosco; Richard de Humez; Hugh Mauleverer; Hugh fitz Hamelin; William Francis; Ingram; Thomas, clerk; and the court of the aforesaid Robert de Brus of Annant.
